test_scenarios.given: An ECS task definition with network_mode set to 'awsvpc' (not host)
test_scenarios.then: The policy should PASS because non-host network modes are not restricted

test_scenarios.given: An ECS task definition with network_mode set to 'bridge' (not host)
test_scenarios.then: The policy should PASS because non-host network modes are not restricted

test_scenarios.given: An ECS task definition with host network mode and a container with privileged=true
test_scenarios.then: The policy should PASS because explicit privileged=true is a secure configuration

test_scenarios.given: An ECS task definition with host network mode and a container with user set to 'appuser' (non-root)
test_scenarios.then: The policy should PASS because non-root user is a secure configuration

test_scenarios.given: An ECS task definition with host network mode and a container with user set to '1000' (numeric UID)
test_scenarios.then: The policy should PASS because numeric non-zero UID is a non-root user

test_scenarios.given: An ECS task definition with host network mode and a container with privileged=false and user='root'
test_scenarios.then: The policy should FAIL because the container runs as root without elevated privileges

test_scenarios.given: An ECS task definition with host network mode and a container with privileged=false and no user specified
test_scenarios.then: The policy should FAIL because the container lacks both privileged and non-root user configuration

test_scenarios.given: An ECS task definition with host network mode and a container with no privileged field and user='root'
test_scenarios.then: The policy should FAIL because privileged defaults to false and user is root

test_scenarios.given: An ECS task definition with host network mode and a container with no privileged field and no user field
test_scenarios.then: The policy should FAIL because both privileged and user are missing (defaults are insecure)

test_scenarios.given: An ECS task definition with host network mode and multiple containers where one is secure and one is insecure
test_scenarios.then: The policy should FAIL because ANY insecure container causes the entire task definition to fail

test_scenarios.given: An ECS task definition with host network mode and multiple containers where all are secure (mix of privileged=true and non-root users)
test_scenarios.then: The policy should PASS because ALL containers have secure configurations
